auth.magic_link_sent: Magic link sent! Check your email to sign in.

rules.title: Trip Rules
rules.subtitle: Please read the following rules carefully. These rules ensure a smooth, spiritually uplifting, and organized trip for everyone.
rules.rule1_desc: All men are required to join the congregation (Jama'at) for prayers at scheduled stops. Praying together strengthens the bonds of brotherhood, brings 27 times more spiritual reward, and anchors our trip in mindfulness and gratitude to Allah.
rules.rule2_desc: Playing music is strictly prohibited during the trip. Keeping the environment free of music allows everyone to relax, reflect on the beautiful Norwegian nature, listen to beneficial reminders, or converse with one another peacefully.
rules.rule3_desc: Protecting the honor of our fellow participants is essential. By strictly avoiding negative talk and backbiting, we keep our hearts clean, invite Allah's mercy, prevent drama, and ensure a stress-free environment for everyone.
rules.rule4_desc: Verbal complaining to other participants is prohibited as it dampens group morale. If you have any issues or concerns, they must be submitted in writing to the designated feedback person. This ensures problems are resolved constructively and professionally.
rules.rule5_desc: This trip is a chance to build lifelong bonds. We expect everyone to spread positivity, help one another, and show patience. These virtues elevate the experience from a simple vacation to a deeply uplifting and unforgettable journey.
rules.rule6_desc: Travel can be stressful — delays, tight spaces, and fatigue can test tempers. This rule encourages every participant to hold their composure and respond with patience and grace rather than frustration.
rules.rule6_quran: "Those who spend in prosperity and adversity, who repress anger, and who pardon people — Allah loves the doers of good." — Surah Aal-Imran 3:134
rules.rule7_desc: If Allah has fixed times for prayer, then respecting schedules is a form of discipline. When the bus departs at a set time, everyone must be present. One person's lateness affects all 17 travelers.
rules.rule7_quran: "Indeed, prayer has been decreed upon the believers at specified times." — Surah An-Nisa 4:103
rules.rule8_desc: Norway's tourist areas will have mixed crowds. This rule reminds participants to maintain Islamic conduct and modesty in public spaces, preserving the spiritual atmosphere of the trip.
rules.rule8_quran: "Tell the believing men to lower their gaze and guard their modesty. That is purer for them." — Surah An-Nur 24:30
rules.rule9_desc: Spend wisely on food, souvenirs, and extras. The trip has a shared budget, and individual extravagance can create tension or envy within the group. Be mindful and moderate in all spending.
rules.rule9_quran: "Eat and drink, but be not excessive. Indeed, He does not like those who commit excess." — Surah Al-A'raf 7:31
rules.rule10_desc: The Sprinter bus and shared accommodations must be kept clean. Each person is responsible for their own area. A tidy space keeps everyone comfortable and reflects our values.
rules.rule10_quran: "Indeed, Allah loves those who are constantly repentant and loves those who purify themselves." — Surah Al-Baqarah 2:222
rules.rule11_desc: Express thanks — to Allah for the journey, to the organizers for planning, and to each other for companionship. Gratitude transforms the entire mood of the group and invites more blessings.
rules.rule11_quran: "If you are grateful, I will surely increase you [in favor]." — Surah Ibrahim 14:7
rules.rule12_desc: If you have nothing beneficial to say, it is better to stay silent. No harsh jokes, sarcasm, or hurtful remarks. Every word should build up, not tear down.
rules.rule12_quran: "And speak to people good words." — Surah Al-Baqarah 2:83
rules.sign_agree: I have read carefully and agree to uphold all rules
rules.sigLockedDesc: Please read and agree to all the covenant rules by checking off all checkboxes to unlock the signature box.
rules.promiseText: We promise, for the sake of Allah, to preserve prayer, good character, patience, gratitude, and love during this journey, and to make this trip a source of barakah and beautiful memories.
rules.agreeCheckbox: I agree to uphold this covenant for myself and my family
rules.typeNameToSign: ✍️ Please type your full name to sign:
rules.signedDesc: JazakAllah Khair. You have officially agreed to the trip rules.
rules.submitFailed: Failed to submit agreement. Please try again.

items.c1_desc: A high-quality windproof and waterproof jacket. Mountain weather is highly unpredictable.
items.c2_desc: Sturdy, waterproof trail boots with good ankle support for glacier and ravine stops.
items.c3_desc: Merino wool or warm base layers (top and bottom). Temperatures can drop below 8°C in passes.
items.c4_desc: Keeps feet warm and dry even if damp. Pack at least 3-4 pairs.
items.s1_desc: Lightweight, waterproof pocket prayer mat for praying in scenic outdoor locations.
items.s2_desc: A small spray bottle to easily perform wudu inside the Sprinter bus or on the road.
items.s3_desc: For daily spiritual recitations and travel reflections.
items.e1_desc: At least 15,000 mAh to keep your phone charged. GPS and camera drain battery quickly.
items.e2_desc: For keeping water or tea hot during long driving days.
items.e3_desc: Provides neck support during the multi-hour Sprinter minibus drives.
items.h1_desc: Crucial for the winding hairpin turns of Trollstigen and Eagle Road.
items.h2_desc: High-factor UV protection. Glaciers and snowy peaks amplify solar rays intensely.
items.h3_desc: For quick hygiene, cleaning hands on the road, and wudu cleanups.
items.c5_desc: Waterproof over-trousers for hiking in rain. Norwegian mountain weather changes fast.
items.c6_desc: Temperatures drop sharply at mountain passes and glacier viewpoints. Pack lightweight merino.
items.c7_desc: UV protection is essential near glaciers, snow, and open fjords. Polarised lenses recommended.
items.s4_desc: For finding prayer direction in remote mountain locations where signal may be weak.
items.s5_name: Dua Book / Fortress of the Muslim
items.s5_desc: Collection of daily duas and travel supplications. Essential for morning/evening adhkar.
items.e4_desc: Multi-port car charger for the Sprinter minibus. Keep all devices topped up during long drives.
items.e5_desc: Essential for evening prayers outdoors, cabin walks at night, and emergency situations.
items.h4_desc: Any prescription meds, inhalers, or EpiPens. Always carry in hand luggage, not checked bags.
items.h5_desc: Midges and mosquitoes near fjords and valleys are common in Norwegian summer.
items.h6_desc: Plasters, antiseptic wipes, paracetamol, ibuprofen, and blister pads for hiking.
items.d1_desc: Required for all participants. Keep accessible for hotel check-ins and emergencies.
items.d2_desc: European Health Insurance Card (EHIC) or travel insurance document. Essential for medical coverage.
items.d3_desc: Required if you're one of the designated drivers. Must be valid in Norway.
items.d4_desc: Have some Norwegian kroner (NOK) as backup. Not all mountain shops accept cards.
items.f1_desc: Stay hydrated on hikes and drives. Norway has excellent, clean tap water everywhere.
items.f2_desc: Dates, nuts, energy bars, and fruit for long driving stretches between stops.
items.f3_desc: For carrying essentials (water, snacks, prayer mat) during hikes and sightseeing stops.
items.f4_desc: For separating wet clothes, dirty laundry, or muddy shoes. Pack 5-10 bags.

checklist.title: Packing Checklist
checklist.subtitle: Interactive list of mandatory and recommended items for the road trip.
checklist.bannerTip1: XXL / Sport 1 / Stormberg: Best for high-quality outdoor rainwear, hiking boots, and wool base layers.
checklist.bannerTip2: Clas Ohlson / Biltema / Kjell & Co: Best for affordable electronics (power banks), thermos flasks, and water spray bottles.
checklist.bannerTip3: Apotek 1 / Vitusapotek: Best for motion sickness pills (essential for Trollstigen) and high-factor sunscreen.
checklist.bannerTip4: Kiwi / Rema 1000 / Coop: Best for daily grocery packages and snacks.

finances.subtitle: Track your payments, trip costs, and verified budget evidence transparently.
finances.transferPrompt: Please transfer your outstanding share before departure.
finances.expandInstruction: Click on any category below to expand itemized calculations, formulas, and verified official booking/pricing sources.
finances.mathematicalProofDesc: We prove that the total group cost is distributed with absolute mathematical parity. The sum of all individual shares matches the group budget exactly:
finances.verifiedBalance: Verified Group Cost Balance: {{total1}} NOK / {{total2}} NOK (100% Balanced)
finances.desktopVippsPrompt: Since you are on a desktop browser, please scan this code or perform a manual transfer in the Vipps app.
finances.scanCodePrompt: Scan code from your phone's camera

itinerary.title: Itinerary & Schedule
itinerary.subtitle: Oslo → Geilo → Stryn → Valldal → Geiranger
itinerary.travelerRulesDesc: Fajr is calculated exactly **1 hour before sunrise** each day. Zuhr/Asr combined at Gol/Bøyabreen (14:00-15:00). Maghrib/Isha combined at **exact local sunset** (approx. 22:45-23:30).
itinerary.clickPointHint: 👉 Click point to scroll to details
itinerary.mapInstructions: Drag to pan. Pinch/scroll to zoom. Hover and click points to navigate details list below.

complaints.subtitle: Submit concerns securely and privately.
complaints.noticeDesc: Your concerns will only be reviewed by the designated Complaint Handler. Other participants cannot see your submissions.
complaints.submittedDesc: Thank you for letting us know. The Complaint Handler will review your concern and take appropriate action.
complaints.submitFailed: Failed to submit complaint. Please try again.

registration.title: Title
registration.passcodeHint: Used to log back in quickly on a new device.
registration.toggleLogin: Already have an account? Log In
registration.phoneError: Please include your country code starting with + (e.g. +47).
registration.identifierError: Please provide either a Phone Number or an Email.

roles.none: None - but I will make Dua
roles.fajr_caller: Wake-Up Caller for Fajr (Wakes up everyone)
roles.lost_found: Lost & Found Custodian (Keeps track of items)

role_hints.none: Supports the group through prayers and positive encouragement without a specific physical duty.
role_hints.fajr_caller: Gently wakes everyone at Fajr time, especially when the summer nights are short and sleep is deep.
role_hints.quran_teacher: Gathers a small circle at rest stops for a 5-minute tadabbur of one ayah related to journeying or creation.
role_hints.grocery_scout: At supermarket stops, checks ingredients for hidden haram additives and picks up fresh halal supplies.
role_hints.hydration_helper: Ensures everyone's water bottles are filled with safe drinking water at each stop, and reminds the group to drink regularly.
role_hints.iftar_announcer: If the trip includes fasting days, announces when it's time to break the fast or stop eating, and signals meal readiness.
role_hints.snack_distributor: Slices fruit, distributes dates, and hands out healthy travel snacks to keep energy levels high during the drive.
role_hints.cleanliness_officer: Maintains cleanliness inside the Sprinter bus by organizing floor sweeps and wiping down tray tables at overnight stops.
role_hints.trash_helper: Collects wrappers and empty bottles from passengers during the drive, and empties trash bags at rest stops.
role_hints.audio_manager: Curates spiritually uplifting nasheeds, Quran recitations, and lectures, and keeps volume at a respectful level.
role_hints.weather_commentator: Monitors forecasts and road closure alerts (like Trollstigen or mountain passes), keeping the group informed.
role_hints.elderly_support: Assists older participants with boarding the bus, carrying bags, navigating steep paths, and finding comfortable seating.
role_hints.entertainment_helper: Organizes halal trivia, Islamic quizzes, storytelling, and group-building games to keep long hours on the bus engaging.
role_hints.garment_organiser: Ensures clean prayer mats, prayer garments (for sisters), and clean soil/turbah are readily available when stopping.
role_hints.lost_found: Collects items left behind in cabins, restaurants, or the bus, and returns them to their rightful owners.
role_hints.complaint_incharge: Collects written feedback and complaints constructively, routing them to the trip leaders to maintain group peace.
role_hints.token_collector: Tracks fuel, tolls, cabin keys, and shared snacks, and ensures everyone's token/share is accounted for.
role_hints.driver: Drives the Sprinter minibus safely, adhering to speed limits, rest break requirements, and Norwegian traffic laws.
role_hints.navigator: Sits in the front passenger seat, monitors the GPS route, flags exits, and helps the driver navigate.
role_hints.photographer: Captures beautiful photos of the scenery, group pictures, and candid moments, creating a shared trip album.
role_hints.journalist: Writes a daily or final trip summary capturing key reminders, funny moments, and the spiritual highlights of the journey.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.alarms_desc: Warning chimes play exactly **10 minutes before** major departures and scheduled prayer times on the road.
dashboard.edit_name_desc: Update how your name appears on the passenger dashboard and allocations.
dashboard.name_required: First and Last name are required.

role_quran.none: "And do good; indeed, Allah loves the doers of good." — Surah Al-Baqarah 2:195
role_quran.fajr_caller: "And enjoin prayer upon your family and be steadfast therein." — Surah Ta-Ha 20:132
role_quran.quran_teacher: "The Most Merciful taught the Qur'an." — Surah Ar-Rahman 55:1-2
role_quran.grocery_scout: "O mankind, eat from whatever is on earth [that is] lawful and good..." — Surah Al-Baqarah 2:168
role_quran.hydration_helper: "...and We made from water every living thing. Will they not then believe?" — Surah Al-Anbya 21:30
role_quran.iftar_announcer: "...And eat and drink until the white thread of dawn becomes distinct to you from the black thread [of night]. Then complete the fast until the sunset..." — Surah Al-Baqarah 2:187
role_quran.snack_distributor: "O believers! Eat of the good things that We have provided for you, and be grateful to Allah..." — Surah Al-Baqarah 2:172
role_quran.cleanliness_officer: "...Indeed, Allah loves those who are constantly repentant and loves those who purify themselves." — Surah Al-Baqarah 2:222
role_quran.trash_helper: "...And clean My House for those who perform Tawaf and those who stand..." — Surah Al-Hajj 22:26
role_quran.audio_manager: "And when the Qur'an is recited, then listen to it and pay attention that you may receive mercy." — Surah Al-A'raf 7:204
role_quran.weather_commentator: "He is the One Who shows you the lightning, inspiring fear and hope..." — Surah Ar-Ra'd 13:12
role_quran.elderly_support: "And your Lord has decreed... to parents, good treatment. Whether one or both of them reach old age..." — Surah Al-Isra 17:23
role_quran.entertainment_helper: "Say, 'In the bounty of Allah and in His mercy - in that let them rejoice; it is better than what they accumulate.'" — Surah Yunus 10:58
role_quran.garment_organiser: "O children of Adam, take your adornment at every masjid..." — Surah Al-A'raf 7:31
role_quran.lost_found: "Indeed, Allah commands you to render trusts to whom they are due..." — Surah An-Nisa 4:58
role_quran.complaint_incharge: "...Who repress anger and who pardon people - and Allah loves the doers of good." — Surah Aal-Imran 3:134
role_quran.token_collector: "And cooperate in righteousness and piety, but do not cooperate in sin and aggression..." — Surah Al-Ma'idah 5:2
role_quran.driver: "Exalted is He who has subjected this to us, and we could not have [otherwise] subdued it. And indeed, to our Lord we will return." — Surah Az-Zukhruf 43:13-14
role_quran.navigator: "And landmarks. And by the stars they are [also] guided." — Surah An-Nahl 16:16
role_quran.photographer: "So observe the effects of the mercy of Allah - how He gives life to the earth after its lifelessness..." — Surah Ar-Rum 30:50
role_quran.journalist: "Nun. By the pen and what they write." — Surah Al-Qalam 68:1
